title: Absolute Maximum and Absolute Minimum
statement: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $f \colon A \to \mathbb{R}$. A point $x^{*} \in A$ is an absolute maximum of $f$ on $A$ if and only if $f(x^{*}) \ge f(x)$ for every $x \in A$. A point $x_{*} \in A$ is an absolute minimum of $f$ on $A$ if and only if $f(x_{*}) \le f(x)$ for every $x \in A$.
